highRatedBusinesses.note: Proportion of Food Standards Agency rated food businesses achieving rating of 4 or 5 (broadly compliant or very good). England, Wales and Northern Ireland.

foodInspectors.note: Estimated number of local authority food safety officers and inspectors, England.

hospitalAdmissions.note: Hospital admissions for food poisoning and foodborne illness, England. ICD-10 codes A00-A09.

sources.name: Food Standards Agency
sources.dataset: Local authority food law enforcement returns

sources.name: NHS England
sources.dataset: Hospital Episode Statistics — admitted patient care

metadata.methodology: FHRS ratings from FSA open data portal. Inspector numbers from LA enforcement returns, converted to full-time equivalent where possible. Hospital admissions from HES using ICD-10 primary diagnosis codes for foodborne gastrointestinal infections. 2020 hospital admission figures reduced by pandemic effect on healthcare-seeking behaviour.
knownIssues: FHRS is mandatory in Wales but voluntary (display) in England — ratings data may undercount non-compliant businesses that decline to display ratings
Inspector numbers conflate dedicated food safety officers with environmental health officers who have wider responsibilities
Hospital admissions substantially undercount true foodborne illness burden — FSA estimates 2.4 million cases annually but most do not present to hospital
